title: The best EVA01 Model
wikitext:
[]: The best EVA01 Model             Quote Post by jolly_kit » Mon Mar 31, 2014 8:53 am Growing up as a kid, i loved NGE, and always told myself when I got the money, I would buy myself the best model I could get. But now that I'm ready to buy, I'm really disappointed with what's out there. It's ironic because apparently everybody complains there are way too many EVA models, but I can't find anything I would be proud to own. http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Tlb4sOZhMpA (Medicom Toy's Real Action Hero NEO EVA - 01 Test Type ) This seems like the best thing out there, but it's pretty disappointing compared to this: At the very least, I'd like a model with articulating hands.. Preferably based on the original series, as I feel the broader chest/shoulders makes for a far more intimidating vibe, eg: [url]http://www.collectiondx.com/toy_review/2007/evangelion_unit01[/url] The two best seem to be: [url]http://www.collectiondx.com/toy_review/2013/rah_neo_evangelion01_test_type[/url] [url]http://www.collectiondx.com/toy_review/2007/evangelion_unit01[/url] (The old PG sounds cool, but apparently it's just plain old and not recommended/worth it) So, I'm deferring to you guys out there that may own these models/have heaps more experience than me, what should I get?[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Mar 31, 2014 9:12 am Comparing Eva and Gundams for toys and models is like apples and handguns. One is a machine that can be easily broken down into distinct parts while the other is a biological system that's far harder to disassemble. Gundams are lots of boxes and parts while Evas are more wholesome and the most you can do is break down the bits of armor. MG or PG-ifying an Eva to Gundam levels would be going too far off from the original designs. IMO articulated hands are a worthless gimmick, especially on smaller scales. Due to the minimum tolerances required for strength the hands often end up disproportionally large and still can't hold any equipment. I would rather have a great set of pre-sculpted hands. Get the RAH if you can afford it and if you can find out without scalping. Otherwise I would recomment the new Kotobukiya kit. http://www.1999.co.jp/eng/10233439 It is both beautifully sculpted and well engineered. Specifically for kits (vs toys) it is the best offering there is. Comes with a whole tree of great pre-sculpted hands

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Mon Mar 31, 2014 9:20 am ^What TMB said. The Bandai LMHG plamo of the original series Shogouki is pretty good, but will require a lot of work, mainly due to its age.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Mar 31, 2014 9:22 am I've been pretty satisfied with the Tamashii SPEC Evas, and there is also PG Unit 01 out there for people who absolutely must have one. RAH is probably the way to go, though.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Mar 31, 2014 9:27 am The PG is worse than the devil. It has rubber pieces for the body and limbs inside which you must insert the frame parts and then over top of it assemble the outside armor. The rubber will fight you every step of the way. It is evil. EVIL I TELL YOU. Also yes for Bandai TV kit. Sure it's old but it's beautiful. If you want ye olde Eva, get that. If you want glorious new movie Eva, get Kotobukiya.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Mon Mar 31, 2014 9:36 am I have heard the PG is a bloody nightmare to assemble, and despite the theoretically good articulation you can't use most of it due to the rubber. I wonder if you could shave down the inside of the rubber suit, or soften it somehow? That said, when it's done it looks beautiful- it even has the capability for lighting. Just don't expect to be able to pose it much.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Mar 31, 2014 9:40 am I have a PG Eva kit. I bought it for $20 from a guy who got massively frustrated with it and when the rubber part tore on him he gave up. That's how evil it is. From 10,000 yen down to $20. EDIT: fixed yen price. Also he threw in an extra Bandai 02 TV kit, spare parts from which have come in handy.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Mon Mar 31, 2014 9:41 am Oh dear. Methinks I'll skip that one.

[]:             Quote Post by jolly_kit » Mon Mar 31, 2014 9:45 am @Bagheera hmm, what is the "tamashii spec"? [url]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LugQghzZ8Dg[/url] Is this what you were referring to? It actually seems really nice, but not sure how I feel about how shiny it is. @Squigsquasher [url]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EOFI_y07HYU[/url] Is this the LM-HG you were referring to? Doesn't seem quite what I'm looking for, doesn't seem like the greatest kit? @TMB I feel you about the poor gundam/eva comparison, but I'd still like for an eva unit with all the bells and whistles: light up eyes articulating fingers light-up S2 engine proper functioning shoulder prog-knife unit If the RAH was based on the TV EVA I probably would've bought it by now, but I'm still unsure how I feel about the movie redesign, they seem overly-stylized and I definitely feel the TV version is more raw/animalistic Currently tossing between the XS-01R or the Medicom EVA01 (RAH) Are there any other models I should scope out?

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Mon Mar 31, 2014 9:55 am The Perfect Grade Unit 01 is the only Eva figure with electronics (and even then you have to install them yourself). The shoulder icons and core can be rigged with LEDs. The eyes can't though. Also it's a terrible model with a goddawful rubber suit thingy. Lots of figures (including the Tamashii SPEC figures) have working prog knife deployment. However, to the best of my knowledge they all use a "dummy knife" with no blade to slot into the pylon, and a seperate knife for use in the figure's hands. No Eva figure has articulated hands. At all. The figure with have to be absolutely massive (like, human sized) to have articulated finger that didn't look like shit. I'm afraid that's one thing you will have to compromise on.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Mon Mar 31, 2014 2:59 pm I will try to find the Robot Tamashii Eva-01 at a manga convention. That one is the best, I've heard.

But now that I'm ready to buy, I'm really disappointed with what's out there. It's ironic because apparently everybody complains there are way too many EVA models, but I can't find anything I would be proud to
